name: Pita Bread
recipeIngredient: About 4 cups unbleached all-purpose or bread flour
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, sprinkle yeast over warm water and stir to dissolve.
2. Add whole wheat flour, one cup at a time, then 1 cup white flour. Stir 100 times in the same direction to activate gluten.
3. Let rest for at least 10 minutes or up to 2 hours.
4. Sprinkle salt over the sponge and stir in olive oil. Mix well.
5. Add white flour, one cup at a time. When dough is too stiff to stir, turn out onto a lightly floured board and knead for 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
6. Return dough to oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let rise until doubled in size (about 1.5 hours).
7. Gently punch down the dough. It can be stored refrigerated up to 5 days or baked later.
8. Bring dough to room temperature before baking.
9. Place unglazed quarry tiles, a large baking stone, or several baking sheets on a rack in the bottom third of the oven (450°F), leaving space for air circulation.
10. Divide dough in half, then into eight pieces. Roll each piece to a circle about 8-9 inches in diameter and less than 1/4 inch thick.
11. Cover rolled-out breads but do not stack. Bake 2 at a time until they puff up or turn lightly golden (3-4 minutes).
12. If not fully puffed, the bread will still be tasty.
